title: Your Python Journey
description: What to learn next and resources.

slides.title: Congratulations! 🎉
slides.content:
# You Did It!

You've learned Python fundamentals:

* ✅ Variables & Data Types
* ✅ Control Flow
* ✅ Functions
* ✅ Data Structures
* ✅ OOP
* ✅ File Handling

You're a Python programmer now!

slides.title: What's Next? 🚀
slides.content:
# Continue Your Journey

**Web Development:**
* Django, Flask, FastAPI

**Data Science:**
* NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib

**Machine Learning:**
* Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch

**Automation:**
* Selenium, BeautifulSoup

slides.title: Build Projects! 🔨
slides.content:
# Learn by Doing

Project ideas:

* 🎮 Simple text games
* 📝 Todo list app
* 🤖 Discord/Telegram bot
* 📊 Data analysis script
* 🌐 Personal website (Flask)
* 🔄 Automation scripts

quizData.question: What's the best way to learn programming?
quizData.explanation: Building projects is the best way to solidify your skills!

slides.title: Resources 📚
slides.content:
# Keep Learning

**Official:**
* Python.org documentation
* PEP documents

**Practice:**
* LeetCode, HackerRank
* Codewars, Exercism

**Community:**
* Stack Overflow
* Reddit r/Python
* Discord servers

slides.title: Python Zen 🧘
slides.content:
# The Zen of Python

```
import this
```

*Beautiful is better than ugly.*
*Simple is better than complex.*
*Readability counts.*
*There should be one obvious way to do it.*

slides.title: Final Tip 🌟
slides.content:
## Never Stop Learning!

* Code every day (even 15 minutes)
* Read other people's code
* Contribute to open source
* Teach others what you learn
* Embrace errors — they're teachers!

**Happy coding! 🐍**

quizData.question: What's most important in your Python journey?
quizData.explanation: Consistent practice and building projects is key to growth!
